text: The application's code structure should be designed for easy extension, facilitating the addition of new features.
The application should be designed for easy porting between iOS and Android platforms.
The search feature should be prominent and easy to find for the user.
The different search options should be evident, simple and easy to understand.
Users should be able to easily understand and select results from the list with just a single click.
Map results should be clear and user-friendly, allowing selection of any pin with a single click.
The information link for each result should be clear, easy to identify, and open with just a single click.
The user interface should be GUI based.
The application itself will only have minimal logic and so there should be little to no issues with the computation required by the phone itself.
The only hazard is a user using the device when they should not be, such as while driving.

The primary attribute of this application will be usability given the large amounts of data and information that will be presented on such a small screen, as well as the user’s ability to input data into the device in a reasonable manner that should not be that much more difficult than if they were at an actual computer.
The FAU class ensures security-relevant events are logged and identifies what information should be included in those logs.
Ideally, each error message will be accompanied by explanatory text and an indication of the action(s) which the user can take in response to the error.
The system should be able to handle parameter changes in three ways: single parameter, subset of settings, and pre-defined parameter set switching.
User interface should be attractive and easily understandable, requiring no assistance to use the website.
System should be able to operate efficiently on slow connections, ensuring fast and steady data flow.
The user interface should prioritize simplicity and avoid cluttering with excessive information to enhance usability.
Data of the user should be regularly synchronized between their device and the database for auto save functionality.
Website should be multi-platform, capable of running on different operating systems.
Users are advised to change their password every 30 days, and maximum 3 login attempts should be allowed.
User details and data should be stored securely in a database provided by the server.
The system should perform its intended function without failure for a specified period.
The passport management website should provide maximal security.
Adaptive Maintenance: The software should be compatible with all web-browsing capable browsers.
The application should be installable on Microsoft or Unix platforms and compatible with browsers such as IE, Firefox, Chrome, Opera, or Safari.
The system should maintain high availability, ensuring uninterrupted operation at all times.
The site should be usable anywhere with internet connectivity and a compatible device.
The application should be available at all working hours, with maintenance or backup operations conducted outside of working hours.
Backup operations should be executed periodically to ensure data safety.
Queries exceeding 1 minute should be terminated.
The system should be designed to facilitate future evolution and maintenance operations.
The website must be capable of storing all data formats and should frequently connect to the database or be database sensitive.
The system should run on a good working PC that meets the hardware interface requirements specified in the system documentation.
The system should be secure to protect user data and prevent unauthorized access.
The system should be responsive and handle edits and playback smoothly.
The system should be easy to learn and use for users with varying levels of musical experience.
The graphics card should have at least 1 GB of VRAM and support OpenGL version 4.0 for enabling GPU Performance (optional feature for enhanced performance with graphic-intensive tasks).
The graphics card should have at least 1 GB of VRAM for optimal performance (optional).
The graphics card should ensure smoother and faster operation for graphic rendering and complex vector calculations (benefit).
The system should be accessible on various devices (mobile app implies mobile accessibility).
The system should avoid becoming overloaded and should maintain optimal performance even under peak load conditions.
The system should efficiently handle medium-sized information databases without significant performance degradation.
The system is not designed for emergency medical use; in case of an emergency, users should contact emergency services such as 108 or their primary doctor.
